source: WHO + CDC Viral Hepatitis & CLD Summary

chunks.text: Liver disease encompasses any condition that causes liver inflammation or tissue damage, potentially leading to cirrhosis, liver failure, or hepatocellular carcinoma.
Chronic liver disease affects millions worldwide, with Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Steatotic Liver Disease (MASLD) becoming the fastest-growing subtype due to rising global obesity rates.
Patients may experience jaundice (yellowing of eyes/skin), abdominal pain and swelling, dark urine color, and chronic fatigue or itchy skin.
Major causes include viral hepatitis (A, B, C), chronic alcohol consumption, and metabolic factors leading to non-alcoholic fatty liver buildup.
Clinicians use Liver Function Tests (LFTs) to measure enzymes, along with imaging like ultrasound or FibroScan, and occasionally a tissue biopsy.
Treatment focuses on the cause: antivirals for hepatitis, lifestyle interventions for fatty liver, or corticosteroids for autoimmune cases. End-stage disease requires liver transplantation.
Key actions include vaccination against Hepatitis A and B, limiting alcohol intake, and maintaining metabolic health through diet to prevent fatty liver accumulation.
